A hurricane is a huge, slow, long-lasting storm that forms over warm ocean water and can span hundreds of miles, while a tornado is a small, violent, short-lived funnel of rotating air over land; hurricanes are far larger but tornadoes pack faster local winds.

WiFi works by sending data as radio waves between a wireless router and your device; the router connects to the internet by cable and broadcasts on set frequencies (2.4 and 5 GHz) that your device's antenna receives and decodes.

The French Revolution (1789-1799) overthrew the French monarchy and feudal privilege, driven by inequality, debt, and Enlightenment ideals; it produced the Declaration of the Rights of Man, the Reign of Terror, and ultimately Napoleon's rise to power.

Deflation is a sustained fall in the general price level, the opposite of inflation; it can be dangerous because people delay purchases expecting lower prices, which slows the economy and raises the real weight of debt.

A comet is an icy body that grows a glowing tail of gas and dust when it nears the Sun, while an asteroid is a rocky or metallic body with no tail; most asteroids orbit between Mars and Jupiter and comets come from the icy outer solar system.
Onions make you cry because cutting them releases a sulfur compound into the air that reacts with the moisture in your eyes to form a mild acid; your eyes water to flush the irritant away.

A tariff is a tax a government places on imported goods, making foreign products more expensive to protect domestic industry or raise revenue; tariffs can also raise prices for consumers and trigger trade wars.

Anesthesia works by using drugs to block nerve signals: local anesthesia numbs a small area, while general anesthesia acts on the brain to produce unconsciousness, stillness, and loss of pain and memory during surgery.

A fever is caused by the immune system raising the body's temperature set-point to fight infection; chemicals called pyrogens tell the brain's hypothalamus to warm the body, which slows pathogen growth and speeds immune response.

The placebo effect is feeling better from a treatment with no active ingredient because you expect to improve, while the nocebo effect is the opposite — feeling worse purely from expecting harm; both reveal the mind's influence over the body.

A seed grows into a plant through germination: it soaks up water and swells, the embryo's root grows down for water and anchorage, the shoot pushes up toward light, and once leaves open the plant photosynthesizes to feed itself.

A republic is a state where power rests with elected representatives and a non-monarch head of state, while a democracy is rule by the people; most modern democracies are also republics that govern through elected representatives under a constitution.

The ocean looks blue because water absorbs the longer red and orange wavelengths of sunlight and scatters the shorter blue wavelengths back to our eyes; deeper, clearer water appears a more intense blue.

The digestive system breaks food into absorbable nutrients: the mouth and stomach break it down mechanically and chemically, the small intestine absorbs the nutrients into the blood, and the large intestine reclaims water before waste leaves the body.

Speed is how fast something moves — a scalar with only magnitude like 60 km/h — while velocity is speed in a specific direction, a vector; a car circling a track at constant speed has constantly changing velocity.

A monopoly exists when a single seller controls an entire market with no real competition, letting it raise prices and cut output; because monopolies reduce choice and efficiency, governments often regulate or break them up.

Antidepressants work mainly by raising the availability of mood-related brain chemicals like serotonin and norepinephrine; SSRIs block serotonin reabsorption, and the benefit builds over weeks as neural circuits gradually adapt.

A crocodile has a longer V-shaped snout and shows teeth from both jaws when its mouth is shut, while an alligator has a wider U-shaped snout that hides its lower teeth; crocodiles handle saltwater better and tend to be more aggressive.

Dark energy is a mysterious form of energy making up about 68 percent of the universe that acts as a repulsive force driving the accelerating expansion of space; it is distinct from dark matter, which adds gravity but does not push space apart.

We cry when sad as an emotional release thought to shed stress hormones and to signal distress to others, drawing comfort and support; emotional tears differ chemically from the basic tears that keep the eyes moist.
